name: La Alpujarra - circular trail running between Pampaneira and Bubión via the Poqueira river
name_original: La Alpujarra – trail running circular entre Pampaneira y Bubión por el río Poqueira
region_intro: Jogging around Pampaneira offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra Nevada National and Natural Park, situated within the Poqueira Gorge. The region features rugged terrain with significant elevation changes, winding paths alongside the Poqueira River, and views of Spain's highest peaks. Lush vegetation, including mulberry and chestnut trees, thrives in the valley, sustained by melting snow from the mountains. The village itself presents steep, labyrinthine streets that connect to a network of trails. Best jogging routes around Pampaneira The most popular jogging route is La Cebadilla Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route features mountain views and leads to a historic ghost village. Another top favourite among local runners is La Alpujarra - circular trail running between Pampaneira and Bubión via the Poqueira river, a difficult 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This circular route offers views of the Poqueira river and connects two villages. Local runners also love the Poqueira River loop from Pampaneira, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through the Poqueira Gorge and alongside the Poqueira River, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Jogging around Pampaneira is defined by Sierra Nevada mountain vistas, the deep Poqueira Gorge, and paths along the Poqueira River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate village paths to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Pampaneira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Pampaneira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Pampaneira?
A: Pampaneira offers a diverse selection of over 40 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. These trails provide access to the stunning landscapes of the Sierra Nevada National and Natural Park.

Q: What do other runners appreciate most about the trails in Pampaneira?
A: The running trails in Pampaneira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 12 reviews. Runners frequently praise the breathtaking Sierra Nevada mountain vistas, the dramatic Poqueira Gorge, and the scenic paths that follow the Poqueira River.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Pampaneira?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are 3 easier jogging routes available in Pampaneira. These typically involve less elevation gain and can be a great way to explore the village's charming streets or gentler paths along the river.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in the Pampaneira area?
A: Absolutely. One popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986556651/la-alpujarra-circular-trail-running-between-pampaneira-and-bubion-via-the-poqueira-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alpujarra - circular trail running between Pampaneira and Bubión via the Poqueira river&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop connecting two villages. The famous "Ruta de los Tres Pueblos" also forms a circuit linking Pampaneira, Bubión, and Capileira.

Q: Can I find running routes that connect Pampaneira with nearby villages?
A: Yes, several routes connect Pampaneira with its picturesque neighbors. The well-known "Ruta de los Tres Pueblos" links Pampaneira with Bubión and Capileira, offering a fantastic way to experience the traditional Alpujarran villages and their surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance running challenges around Pampaneira?
A: For those seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Public Washhouse of Ferreirola – Bubión loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding route stretching over 32 km with considerable elevation changes, perfect for experienced trail runners.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the running trails?
A: The trails in Pampaneira are rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter dramatic mountain vistas, including views of Mulhacén and Veleta peaks, the lush Poqueira Gorge with its winding river, and diverse vegetation like mulberry, chestnut, and holm oak trees. The area also features abundant water channels and fountains, fed by melting Sierra Nevada snow.

Q: Are there any historical or interesting landmarks along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with interesting sights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986567905/la-cebadilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cebadilla Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to a historic ghost village. You can also explore the unique Berber architecture and charming squares within Pampaneira itself, or discover natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Little Lakes of the Virgin&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow the Poqueira River?
A: Yes, the Poqueira River is a prominent feature of the local landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6881465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poqueira River loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt; is a popular trail that leads through the Poqueira Gorge and directly alongside the river, offering beautiful waterside views.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Pampaneira?
A: Pampaneira, located on the southern slopes of the Sierra Nevada, generally offers pleasant conditions for jogging outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while winter can offer crisp air and stunning snow-capped mountain views, though some higher trails might be affected by snow.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging workout?
A: Absolutely. Pampaneira's location in the Sierra Nevada means many routes feature significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6881465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poqueira River loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt; includes over 540 meters of ascent, providing a strenuous workout amidst stunning scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Spain's highest mountains?
A: Yes, many trails in and around Pampaneira offer spectacular views of the Sierra Nevada mountains, including Spain's highest peaks, Mulhacén and Veleta. The dramatic Poqueira Gorge itself provides a foreground to these majestic summits.
tips: This waterfall is a good example of the force with which the waters of the Poqueira River fall, traditionally used for irrigation and today for power generation.
Originated by the confluence of multiple streams from Mulhacén and Veleta

El Barranco del Poqueira is one of the most visited natural spaces in the province of Granada. This impressive site on the southern slope of the Sierra Nevada slopes steeply from the summit of Veleta. It includes villages of great beauty on its mountain slopes: Bubión is one of them. The village was put forward by the Junta de Andalucía (Andalusian government). This is how it has become over the years, a choice of prime importance for tourism in the region.
